description: Backend database schema for a personal study workspace application with user authentication, subject/topic organization, notes, PDF materials, and code snippets.

avatar_styles.description: Master table for predefined animated SVG avatar styles

name.description: Avatar style name (e.g., 'Floating Orb', 'Bouncing Ball', 'Rotating Cube')

svg_template.description: SVG template with animation (can include CSS animations and variables)

color_customizable.description: Whether avatar colors can be customized

is_active.description: Whether this avatar style is available for selection

avatar_colors.description: Master table for avatar color customization options

is_active.description: Whether this color is available for selection

color_palette.description: Master table for subject and note color themes

description.description: Description of the color palette theme

palette_type.description: Whether palette is for subjects, notes, or both

color_name.description: Name of the color in this palette (e.g., 'Linen', 'Rust')

tint_palette.description: Master table for note/topic tint colors (warm/dusty palette)

password_hash.description: Bcrypt hashed password (never store plain text)

avatar_style_id.description: Reference to selected animated SVG avatar style

avatar_color_id.description: Reference to selected avatar color for customization

subjects.description: Study subjects (e.g., Data Structures, Operating Systems)

color_id.description: Reference to color from palette master table

topics.description: Topics within subjects (e.g., Graph Algorithms, Dynamic Programming)

tint_id.description: Reference to tint color from master palette

notes.description: Study notes with markdown content and optional scribbles

tint_id.description: Reference to tint color from master palette

scribbles.description: Array of ScribbleStroke objects for hand-drawn annotations

materials.description: Study materials (PDFs, documents) uploaded by users

page_count.description: Number of pages (for PDFs, populated after first open)

storage_key.description: Key to fetch blob from cloud storage (S3, GCS, etc.)

playground_snippets.description: Code snippets for testing and experimentation

user_sessions.description: User authentication sessions (optional, for token-based auth)

ip_address.description: Client IP address for security audit

user_agent.description: Client user agent for security audit

password_reset_tokens.description: Temporary tokens for password reset flow

used_at.description: When token was used (null if unused)

user_to_avatar_style.description: Each user selects one animated SVG avatar style

user_to_avatar_color.description: Each user selects a color to customize their avatar

subject_to_palette_color.description: Each subject optionally references a color from the palette

topic_to_tint.description: Each topic references a tint color from the master palette

note_to_tint.description: Each note references a tint color from the master palette

subject_to_materials.description: A subject has multiple study materials

user_to_snippets.description: A user creates multiple code snippets

note_to_snippets.description: A note can have associated code snippets (optional)

user_to_sessions.description: A user can have multiple active sessions

user_to_reset_tokens.description: A user can have multiple reset tokens

palette_type.description: Type of palette - for subjects, notes, or both

data_types_reference.VARCHAR(n): Variable-length string up to n characters
data_types_reference.JSONB: JSON binary format (PostgreSQL) - supports indexing and querying

master_data_explanation.overview: Master tables provide centralized management of predefined options that users can choose from, ensuring consistency and easy updates across the application.

avatar_styles.purpose: Predefined animated SVG avatar styles that users can choose from
avatar_styles.usage: When users sign up, they select from this palette of animated avatars. The avatar_style_id is stored in the users table.
features: 8 unique animated SVG styles (Floating Orb, Bouncing Ball, Rotating Cube, etc.)
Each style has its own animation type and duration
Supports color customization (most styles are color-customizable)
SVG templates stored in database for easy updates
Animation types: float, bounce, rotate, pulse, wave, morph, particle
benefits: Unique, personalized avatars for each user
Easy to add new avatar styles without code changes
Supports accessibility (can disable animations if needed)
avatar_styles.example_flow: User signs up → Selects avatar style (e.g., 'Floating Orb') → Selects color (e.g., 'Sky Blue') → avatar_style_id and avatar_color_id stored → Avatar rendered with SVG template + selected color

avatar_colors.purpose: Color customization options for animated avatars
avatar_colors.usage: Users select a color to customize their chosen avatar style. The avatar_color_id is stored in the users table.
features: 8 predefined colors (Sky Blue, Coral Pink, Lavender, etc.)
Each color has hex, RGB, and HSL representations
Colors can be applied to any color-customizable avatar style
Easy to enable/disable colors via is_active flag
avatar_colors.example_flow: User selects 'Floating Orb' style → Chooses 'Sky Blue' color → Avatar renders with blue-tinted floating orb animation

color_palette.purpose: Organize colors into themed palettes (e.g., 'Warm Tones', 'Cool Tones', 'Earthy')
color_palette.usage: Subjects can reference colors from these palettes. Allows grouping related colors together.
benefits: Organize colors by theme or mood
color_palette.example_flow: Admin creates 'Warm Tones' palette → Adds 8 colors to it → Users select subject color from palette → color_id stored in subjects table

palette_colors.purpose: Junction table linking colors to palettes
palette_colors.usage: Stores individual colors within each palette with their order and metadata
palette_colors.structure: Many-to-many relationship between color_palette and actual colors
palette_colors.example: Palette 'Warm Tones' contains: Linen (#E8D4C0), Rust (#D97060), Ochre (#D9A860), Clay (#C9906B)

tint_palette.purpose: Master palette of warm/dusty tints specifically for notes and topics
tint_palette.usage: Notes and topics reference tint_id from this table instead of storing raw HSL strings
benefits: Consistent tint colors across all notes/topics
Easy to update all tints globally
tint_palette.example_flow: Note created → System assigns random tint_id from tint_palette → Note stores tint_id → UI fetches HSL from tint_palette table

master_data_explanation.data_consistency: By using master tables, if you want to change a color, you update it once in the master table and all references automatically use the new value.
migration_strategy: 1. Create master tables: avatar_styles, avatar_colors, color_palette, palette_colors, tint_palette
2. Seed avatar_styles with 8 animated SVG templates
4. Add avatar_style_id and avatar_color_id columns to users table
5. Migrate existing user data (assign default avatar style and color)
6. Drop old avatar_color column from users
7. Add NOT NULL constraints to new avatar columns

security_considerations.password_storage: Always use bcrypt or similar with salt. Never store plain text passwords.
security_considerations.token_security: Use cryptographically secure random tokens for reset/session tokens.
security_considerations.sql_injection: Use parameterized queries/prepared statements in all database operations.
security_considerations.data_validation: Validate all user inputs on backend before database insertion.
security_considerations.rate_limiting: Implement rate limiting on auth endpoints (sign-up, sign-in, password reset).
security_considerations.cors: Configure CORS properly to prevent unauthorized cross-origin requests.
security_considerations.https: Always use HTTPS in production for all API communications.
security_considerations.audit_logging: Log all authentication events and sensitive operations.
security_considerations.soft_deletes: Consider soft deletes for user data retention and compliance.

performance_recommendations.indexing: Indexes are defined on foreign keys and frequently queried columns
performance_recommendations.pagination: Implement pagination for list endpoints (subjects, topics, notes, materials)
performance_recommendations.caching: Cache user profile and subject/topic hierarchy in Redis
performance_recommendations.query_optimization: Use database query analysis tools to identify slow queries
performance_recommendations.connection_pooling: Use connection pooling (e.g., PgBouncer for PostgreSQL)
performance_recommendations.batch_operations: Support batch operations for bulk note/topic creation

GET /master/avatar-styles.description: List all available animated SVG avatar styles

GET /master/avatar-styles/:id.description: Get specific avatar style with SVG template

GET /master/tint-palette.description: List all available tint colors for notes/topics

request_body.language: string (required, javascript, python, cpp, java, etc.)

example_queries.get_user_dashboard: SELECT s.*, COUNT(DISTINCT t.id) as topic_count, COUNT(DISTINCT n.id) as note_count FROM subjects s LEFT JOIN topics t ON s.id = t.subject_id LEFT JOIN notes n ON t.id = n.topic_id WHERE s.user_id = $1 GROUP BY s.id ORDER BY s.created_at DESC
example_queries.get_topic_with_notes: SELECT t.*, COUNT(n.id) as note_count FROM topics t LEFT JOIN notes n ON t.id = n.topic_id WHERE t.id = $1 GROUP BY t.id
example_queries.search_notes: SELECT n.* FROM notes n JOIN topics t ON n.topic_id = t.id JOIN subjects s ON t.subject_id = s.id WHERE s.user_id = $1 AND (n.title ILIKE $2 OR n.body ILIKE $2) ORDER BY n.updated_at DESC LIMIT 50
example_queries.get_user_progress: SELECT s.id, s.name, COUNT(DISTINCT t.id) as total_topics, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'completed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as completed_topics, AVG(t.confidence) as avg_confidence FROM subjects s LEFT JOIN topics t ON s.id = t.subject_id WHERE s.user_id = $1 GROUP BY s.id

phases.phase_1: Create core tables: users, subjects, topics, notes, materials
phases.phase_3: Add playground_snippets table for code execution feature
phases.phase_5: Add audit logging and soft deletes if needed
